core_mechanism: inferring a linear coupling (proportionality) coefficient between two co-registered random fields — a tracer field and an underlying reference field — from the ratio of their cross-spectrum to the tracer auto-spectrum, using second-order (two-point) correlation statistics as a linear-response estimator
brief_reason: The method estimates a bias coefficient b = P_gg/P_gK from the auto- and cross-power spectra of a tracer field against a reference (lensing) field to recover the underlying field's amplitude, which is exactly a two-point cross-correlation linear-coupling estimator (with a conditional occupation-count / host-assignment model layered on at small scales).
